mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching labor market peak and economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand absorbing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 50 basis points in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth combined with stable consumer price inflation confirms a soft-landing environment, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% and CPI remains below 3%, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation exceeds the Federal Reserve implicit target while policy rates remain constrained, real rates turn negative and trigger a repricing of duration risk, depressing equity valuations within a week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite high inflation and unchanged policy rates, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over inflation alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum and wage-driven consumption, sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of positive payroll growth months, or rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume coupled with increasing housing permits signals synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving earnings revisions upward and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive forward returns when both series rise simultaneously, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratios drop below 0.5 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial output stagnates while unemployment remains elevated indicates structural supply-demand mismatch and capacity utilization decline, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within a week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when industrial production flattens and unemployment rises concurrently, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a 5-year rolling window.
